task_name: Workspace schema and idempotent migration foundation

strengths: Default Workspace creation, per-user personal workspace creation, and legacy data assignment all implemented idempotently in schema.sql:123-161 with an EXISTS(workspaces) guard that makes re-runs a true no-op, matching the pure planner in migrateHelpers.ts:41-43.

Settings re-key handled exactly per plan in 3 guarded steps (ADD COLUMN at schema.sql:115, backfill UPDATE at schema.sql:160, conditional drop single-col PK + SET NOT NULL + composite PK at schema.sql:164-179) with a pg_constraint check so the PK swap only runs once.
NOT NULL on scoped board tables is applied conditionally after backfill (schema.sql:182-195), preventing failure if any unscoped rows remain and keeping the migration restartable.

migrate.ts:25-33 adds the entry-point guard so the module is import-safe in tests; migrateHelpers.ts is a pure function with no pool import or side effects, satisfying the 'importable in tests without side effects' constraint.
No edits to server/src/core/* — the architecture constraint is respected; all 5 changed files are DB-layer only.
Targeted test passes (2/2) and commit f92a453 matches the conventional-commit message 'feat(workspaces): add scoped schema migration'.

issues.description: seed.ts duplicates every INSERT into parallel null-workspace and non-null-workspace branches (columns at lines 68-77, cards/card_events at lines 95-121). Defensible as backward compatibility for a pre-migration DB where no Default Workspace exists, but it is a maintainability smell that doubles the SQL surface. Non-blocking.

Coverage observation: workspaceMigration.test.ts verifies the schema via string/regex matching against schema.sql text and exercises the pure planner only — it does not execute the migration SQL against a database. The DELIVERABLE outcomes (NOT NULL actually enforced, no duplicate workspaces on re-run) are therefore verified by reading the DDL, not by execution. This matches the plan's Step 1 design ('migration SQL is analyzed/applied by the migration helper'), so it is not a spec-compliance failure; recommend an integration run before Phase 2 cutover.
